text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 16 July 2021 

Dr Miriam Patel  
St Bartholomew’s Hospital  
Department of Neurology, West Smithfield, London EC1A 7BE  

Dear Dr Miriam Patel,  

Alex Turner, DOB: 17-03-1992, Hospital No: H493027 NHS No. 9021847365  
Flat 12, 48 Fleet Street, London EC4Y 1BN 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent seizures of undetermined classification 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
Clobazam 10 mg at night when clusters occur (patient-initiated as per prior plan) 

Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
- Maintain patient-led clobazam 10 mg nocte for cluster periods for up to 3 nights, then stop  
- Reinforce seizure first-aid and nocturnal safety measures  
- Keep detailed seizure and sleep diary; bring to next review  
- EEG with sleep-deprivation requested; consider prolonged home video-EEG if events persist  
- Routine review in 4 months or sooner if escalation 

I reviewed Alex Turner in clinic today. They have been practising aura recognition strategies as discussed previously and are able to describe their warning features in their own words as a brief rising internal sensation accompanied by a metallic taste and a momentary feeling of detachment, typically lasting under 30 seconds. They report improved confidence in identifying these early signs and implementing their pre-agreed safety plan at bedtime.

Regarding recent activity, they describe 3 nocturnal clusters this month; each ~two to four events. These occurred between 01:00 and 04:00, with events separated by 10–30 minutes. Post-event confusion was mild and self-limiting. There were no witnessed generalised convulsions, tongue biting, or urinary incontinence reported this month. Triggers identified by the patient include two nights of curtailed sleep and a period of work-related stress. Alcohol intake remains minimal. There have been no daytime episodes in the same period.

They have used clobazam 10 mg at night at the onset of cluster patterns, for up to three consecutive nights, which they feel reduces subsequent events and improves recovery the following day. No adverse effects beyond transient morning grogginess have been noted. Adherence to levetiracetam remains good, and they deny mood change or irritability beyond baseline.

Examination today was unremarkable. We discussed ongoing nocturnal safety, including avoiding sleeping at height, placing a low-profile bed barrier, and ensuring a partner is aware of first-aid steps. We also reviewed the diary format to distinguish single nights with multiple brief events from prolonged episodes. I have requested a sleep-deprived EEG to better characterise the events and will escalate to prolonged home video-EEG if nocturnal clustering continues despite the current plan.

I will review Alex Turner in four months. They will contact the service sooner if there is any increase in severity, emergence of daytime convulsions, injury, or treatment intolerance.

With best wishes,

entities.text: they describe 3 nocturnal clusters this month; each ~two to four events.

attributes.Label: 3 cluster per month, 2 to 4 per cluster

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "they describe 3 nocturnal clusters this month; each ~two to four events." This provides both the number of clusters over a month and the number of events per cluster. There are no other conflicting frequencies mentioned. Therefore, the normalized label should be in the cluster format with month as the period and 2 to 4 per cluster.
